li.p: The function is quadratic, so the domain is {\left(-\infty ,\infty \right)}\text{.} There are no invalid inputs for this function.

Since the formula is in vertex form, we can see that the graph of G has a vertex at {\left(-6,-6\right)}\text{.} Since the leading coefficient is 3 which is positive, the parabola opens upward. So the range is all numbers greater than or equal to -6\text{.} So the range is {\left[-6,\infty \right)}\text{.}
